behavior_summary: Provides 'U8P_LCHST_NS::look' and 'use' handlers for a locked chest item. The 'U8P_LCHST_NS::look' handler causes the item to display a short description ('locked chest ') and then yields/suspends. The 'use' handler either spawns the 'U8P_LCHST_NS::look' handler when the main game loop is not running or, when the game is running, plays a sound and toggles the chest UI (gump) open/closed based on a status bit.

The chest uses Item::bark to present short text to the player, isGameRunning to decide between spawn vs. immediate behavior, and Item::getStatus to test a 0x0100 flag that determines whether Item::openGump or Item::closeGump is called.

When the game is not running, U8P_LCHST_NS::use delegates to U8P_LCHST_NS::look via spawn so the same 'locked chest' message appears without opening the gump; when running it provides audible feedback (playSFX) and toggles UI state.
notable_control_flow: U8P_LCHST_NS::use checks isGameRunning(): if false then spawn U8P_LCHST_NS::look (passing pid/item) and suspend; if true continue to play sound and query item status.
After playSFX, U8P_LCHST_NS::use calls Item::getStatus() and tests (status & 0x0100): when set -> call Item::openGump(item, 0x07); when clear -> call Item::closeGump(item).

U8P_LCHST_NS::look prepares item/process info, calls Item::bark(item, "locked chest ") to show a message, cleans up the temporary string, executes an implies/pop bookkeeping sequence, then suspends (yields).
open_questions: What exact semantic meaning does the Item status bit 0x0100 have (open flag, locked flag, or 'has-gump-open' state)? — appears to control gump open vs close but exact name is unknown.

What is the intended target of the bark call (the item's owner/player PID vs. global UI)? The code pushes a pid when spawning and passes item to Item::bark; this appears to direct the message to the triggering process but is inferred.
Why does spawn-only behavior occur when the game loop is not running (is this a UI/state limitation of the engine)? — likely an engine-level guard but details are uncertain.
What does the openGump parameter 0x07 represent (gump type, page, or mode)? — inferred to be a gump parameter but meaning is unclear.
What role does the implies/pop sequence have exactly in the suspend/resume flow (bookkeeping for process continuation)?

main_function.name: U8P_LCHST_NS::look
main_function.purpose: Handle the 'U8P_LCHST_NS::look' (examine) interaction for the locked chest: inform the player by barking the literal text "locked chest " and then suspend the handler.
entry_conditions: Event 0: triggered when an actor looks at or inspects the chest (examine/observe interaction).
Handler starts by setting item/process info and excluding the process (engine bookkeeping) before producing feedback.

function_map.name: U8P_LCHST_NS::look
function_map.role: Examination ('U8P_LCHST_NS::look') handler: show item description and yield.

function_map.name: U8P_LCHST_NS::use
function_map.role: Use/activation handler: play SFX and toggle chest UI when game running; spawn U8P_LCHST_NS::look handler when not running.

internal_calls.purpose: Spawn/delegate to the U8P_LCHST_NS::look handler when the game loop is not running so the locked-chest message appears without opening UI.

external_calls.purpose: Display a short description/text associated with the item ('locked chest ') to the relevant process/player.
Decide whether to spawn the U8P_LCHST_NS::look handler (when false) or proceed with immediate use behavior (when true).
Provide audible feedback when the chest is used while the game is running (args observed: 0x64, 0x58).
Retrieve item status flags; tested against mask 0x0100 to decide open vs close behavior.
Open the chest UI/gump when the tested status bit (0x0100) is set; passed an extra parameter 0x07.
Close the chest UI/gump when the tested status bit (0x0100) is clear.

The 'U8P_LCHST_NS::look' handler causes the item to display a short description ('locked chest ') and then yields/suspends. The 'use' handler either spawns the 'U8P_LCHST_NS::look' handler when the main game loop is not running or, when the game is running, plays a sound and toggles the chest UI (gump) open/closed based on a status bit.
